name: Coleman County State Bank
description_short: Community bank in Texas offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and mortgages across 5 locations in Coleman, Abilene, San Angelo, and Santa Anna.
description_long:
Coleman County State Bank is a traditional community bank headquartered in Coleman, Texas, with a physical presence across Central Texas. The bank operates five branch locations in Coleman, Abilene (two locations), San Angelo, and Santa Anna, serving both individual consumers and business customers in rural and semi-rural Texas communities. The bank maintains traditional banking hours with extended drive-thru service at some locations and 24/7 ATM access at multiple sites. The bank was founded with roots in the local community, as evidenced by their acquisition of Santa Anna National Bank, indicating consolidation and expansion within their regional market.

Coleman County State Bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including personal checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), personal loans, and mortgage loans. On the business side, they provide business checking and savings accounts, business loans, agricultural loans, and cash management services. All customers have access to online banking for remote account management. The bank provides ancillary services including notary services, safe deposit boxes, night deposit facilities, and a motor bank drive-thru window at their main Coleman location.

The bank distinguishes itself through its focus on agricultural and business lending, indicated by dedicated "Ag Loans" and "Cash Management" service offerings—services less commonly emphasized by larger regional or national banks. Their multiple branch locations across a tri-county area (Coleman, Taylor, and Tom Green counties) provide physical accessibility for customers in smaller communities where banking options may be limited. The acquisition of Santa Anna National Bank suggests the bank actively pursues growth and consolidation opportunities while maintaining local presence. Their routing number (111321814) and SWIFT code (TIBBUS44) indicate full integration into the Federal Reserve system.

As a traditional community bank, Coleman County State Bank lacks the digital-first features, extensive product diversity, and competitive rates often found at larger institutions or online banks. Customers seeking investment products, wealth management, or advanced financial services would need to look elsewhere. The limited information on their website regarding specific rates, fees, or detailed product terms makes comparative shopping difficult. For customers in their service areas seeking straightforward banking with local decision-making and agricultural lending expertise, the bank serves a clear niche; for others, options may offer better rates or broader services.
services: Personal savings accounts and money market accounts
Mortgage loans (home purchase and refinance implied)
pros: Multiple physical branch locations across Central Texas (Coleman, Abilene, San Angelo, Santa Anna) providing in-person service accessibility
Specialized agricultural lending ("Ag Loans") serving rural and farm-based customers often underserved by national banks
Extended drive-thru hours at Abilene locations (7:30 am - 6 pm weekdays) accommodating working customers
24/7 ATM access at multiple locations including standalone Coleman ATM
Full-service offerings including business loans, cash management, and merchant services for small business owners
Safe deposit boxes and notary services available at multiple branch locations
Established community bank with 24/7 debit card support (800-500-1044) for account issues
cons: Limited weekend hours—only Saturday 9 am-1 pm at Abilene locations; no Sunday lobby service at any branch
Minimal online information about rates, fees, account minimums, or loan terms making price comparison difficult
No mention of mobile app, mobile banking, or advanced digital services on website
Limited geographic footprint (only 5 locations in 4 Texas cities) restricting accessibility for customers outside service area
No information about investment products, wealth management, or advisory services for complex financial needs
best_for: Agricultural businesses and farmers in Coleman, Taylor, and Tom Green counties needing specialized ag lending
Small business owners seeking local decision-making and personalized cash management services
Customers in rural Texas communities where branch accessibility and in-person banking are important
Consumers preferring traditional community bank relationships over national chain or online-only institutions
diagnosis: Coleman County State Bank is best for agricultural businesses, small business owners, and rural customers in Central Texas seeking traditional community banking with local branch access and specialized ag lending. The main caveat is limited digital capabilities, sparse public rate information, and geographic restriction to five locations in Texas, making it unsuitable for customers seeking online-first banking or residing outside their service area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
